what_it_does: Configures Vitest for the recollection package: enables test globals, sets the environment to node, includes tests from test/**/*.test.ts, disables file parallelism, raises test and hook timeouts to 90 seconds, and registers a global setup script to warm a served embedding endpoint before any test runs.
observations: Uses `defineConfig` from `vitest/config` and exports the default config object.
Sets `fileParallelism: false` to serialize test files.
Comments explain the config as a reaction to switching the embed lane from an in-process 384-dim ONNX model to a served HTTP 2560-dim endpoint (referenced as `pplx-ctx-4b-docs`).
Comments mention a cold-model-load of ~16s, 37 test files, per-call latency of ~1–3s, a 15-exchange long-conversation fixture taking ~18.7s to embed serially, and a measured worst case of ~40s.
Comments state the issues were revealed by B-7 and are test-infra fallout, not wiring defects.
standouts: Disabling file parallelism to avoid queuing many test files against a single embedding endpoint.
A global setup hook whose sole purpose is to warm the served embedding endpoint outside timed tests.
Timeouts explicitly sized from a measured worst-case serial embedding latency rather than default values.
Extensive inline narrative justifying each non-default setting.
conventions: Follows the standard Vitest config shape: `defineConfig`, default export, nested `test` object.
Breaks the default parallel test-file execution and short timeouts because of domain-specific external-service latency.
concerns: 90-second timeouts can hide genuine hangs or slow failures.
Serializing all test files will lengthen the overall suite duration.
The rationale is heavily encoded in comments rather than in named config fields or external docs.
The global setup script is referenced but not shown in this file, so its fail-soft behavior is not verifiable here.
Assumptions about endpoint latency and cold-start time are hardcoded in comments and may drift.
notes: This file is less a test config than a latency-mitigation policy for a slow, single-threaded external embedding endpoint.
questions: What is the exact URL and contract of the served embedding endpoint?
What does B-7 refer to in this context?
Do all 37 test files actually exercise the embedding path, or only a subset?
How are timeouts monitored so they do not mask real performance regressions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—adapting Vitest to the latency of a served 2560-dim embedding endpoint—matches the actual settings: serial execution, 90 s timeouts, and a global warmup setup.
apparent_themes: Serialization of a scarce external resource
intention_signals: It is trying to make the recollection test suite reliable after moving embedding from a fast in-process model to a slow served model, by ensuring only one embed call is in flight at a time and by absorbing cold-start latency before timed tests begin.
novelty: Embedding-specific performance narrative embedded directly in a Vitest config; timeouts are explicitly derived from measured serial embedding latency rather than chosen arbitrarily.
contribution: It contributes the Vitest test orchestration policy for the recollection package: serial execution, timeout budgets sized to embedding latency, and a global setup hook to warm the served embedding endpoint.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest configuration for the recollection package that disables file parallelism, sets 90-second test and hook timeouts, and runs a global setup script to warm a served 2560-dimensional embedding endpoint before tests.
intention_for_embedding: This file configures Vitest to serialize tests and warm an external embedding endpoint so that cold-start latency does not make tests flaky after switching from an in-process 384-dim model to a served 2560-dim model.
